title: FSL Knowledge Check
description: Eight questions covering alphabet, numbers, greetings, and everyday phrases.

questions.question: In FSL, the letter A is typically formed by:
options: Fist with thumb on the side

questions.question: How do you sign the number 3 in many sign systems (including common FSL teaching)?
options: Thumb, index, and middle finger extended

questions.question: A common way to sign “thank you” involves:
options: Touching fingers to the chin, then moving the hand forward

questions.question: FSL is best described as:
options: A visual language with its own grammar
Identical to ASL in every region

questions.question: Signing “yes” often resembles:
options: Shaking the head side to side
Nodding the fist up and down

questions.question: The number 5 is often shown by:
When signing “hello,” a common movement is:
options: Hand wave side to side with an open palm

questions.question: Facial expressions in sign languages often:
options: Carry grammar and meaning (e.g., questions, negation)
